Article Title: ‘To pin the blame on him is unfair’: Ashwin defends Hardik Pandya amid MI’s disappointing campaign
Article Text:
Hardik Pandya (Pic Credit: IPL)

‘You’re asking him to do too much’

Concern over Hardik’s batting form

Poll Do you believe Hardik Pandya deserves to be blamed for poor IPL 2026 performance of MI? Yes, he should be accountable No, it's unfair to blame him

NEW DELHI: Ravichandran Ashwin has come out strongly in support of Hardik Pandya , insisting it is unfair to hold the captain solely responsible for Mumbai Indians ’ disappointing IPL 2026 campaign.MI, one of the most successful franchises in IPL history, became only the second side to be knocked out of playoff contention this season after managing just four wins from 12 matches. With Hardik also enduring a lean run with both bat and ball, criticism around his captaincy has intensified.Ashwin, however, believes the blame has been misplaced.“I wouldn't review his captaincy this year at all. Honestly, when you have seasons like this, to pin the blame on the captain is quite unfair. Nobody has turned up. The team has failed to turn up,” Ashwin told ESPNcricinfo.The former India spinner pointed to Hardik’s successful leadership stint at Gujarat Titans, where he led the side to a title and a runners-up finish in two seasons, as proof of his captaincy credentials.“He was there at Gujarat Titans. Had two fabulous seasons. So clearly, as a leader, he did something right there,” Ashwin said.Ashwin added that replacing Rohit Sharma as MI captain in 2024 was never going to be easy.“It’s not easy to replace an incumbent Indian captain, white-ball captain, such as Rohit Sharma. Five titles in the IPL. He’s won a T20 World Cup. You’ve got a lot of fandom going around in the country. You should have a thick skin to survive social media these days.”Ashwin also raised concerns over Hardik’s batting, saying his timing issues suggest there could be an underlying physical issue.“With his batting, I found something quite interesting and strange — how late he’s been on the ball. Is he going through something physically? We wouldn’t know. But he’s been late on hard-length deliveries.”Hardik has scored just 146 runs in eight innings this season, while picking up four wickets at an economy of 11.90. He also missed three matches due to a reported back issue.Still, Ashwin insisted the all-rounder remains invaluable.“Hardik is a once-in-a-generation player. You don’t find such players. If India finds itself in a certain T20 mastery now, Hardik’s been a linchpin in that,” he added.
Article Summary: Ravichandran Ashwin has come out strongly in support of Hardik Pandya. Ashwin believes it is unfair to hold the captain solely responsible for Mumbai Indians’ disappointing IPL 2026 campaign. MI became only the second side to be knocked out of playoff contention after managing just four wins from 12 matches.

Article Title: Updated IPL 2026 Points Table After RCB vs KKR match: RCB go top after Kohli masterclass; KKR playoff hopes fade
Article Text:
Virat Kohli celebrates after scoring his hundred in Raipur. (Pic credit: IPL)

Watch Krunal Pandya opens up on match-winning knock and RCB’s playoffs push Krunal Pandya opens up on match-winning knock and RCB’s playoffs push

IPL 2026 Points Table after RCB vs KKR match

Pos Team P W L NR Pts NRR 1 Royal Challengers Bengaluru 12 8 4 0 16 +1.053 2 Gujarat Titans 12 8 4 0 16 +0.551 3 Sunrisers Hyderabad 12 7 5 0 14 +0.331 4 Punjab Kings 11 6 4 1 13 +0.428 5 Chennai Super Kings 11 6 5 0 12 +0.185 6 Rajasthan Royals 11 6 5 0 12 +0.082 7 Delhi Capitals 12 5 7 0 10 -0.993 8 Kolkata Knight Riders 11 4 6 1 9 -0.198 9 Mumbai Indians (E) 11 3 8 0 6 -0.585 10 Lucknow Super Giants (E) 11 3 8 0 6 -0.907

NEW DELHI: Defending champions Royal Challengers Bengaluru reclaimed top spot in the IPL 2026 points table on Wednesday with a thumping six-wicket victory over Kolkata Knight Riders in Raipur.RCB talisman Virat Kohli played a match-winning unbeaten 105 off 60 balls to help the team chase down 193 in 19.1 overs for their eighth win of the season. They now top the standings in the business end of the IPL 2026 league stage with 16 points and an impressive net run rate (NRR) of +1.053, overtaking Gujarat Titans (16 points) on superior NRR.The defeat left KKR stranded in eighth spot, keeping their playoff hopes alive by the thinnest of margins. KKR’s four-match winning streak ended against RCB despite posting a competitive target.After RCB and GT, Sunrisers Hyderabad and Punjab Kings occupied the remaining two playoff spots with 14 and 13 points respectively.The rest of the standings remain unchanged after Match No. 57 of the 70-match league stage.Chennai Super Kings are fifth with 12 points, ahead of Rajasthan Royals on superior NRR, with both teams having three matches left each.Below them, Delhi Capitals and KKR are still mathematically alive in the playoff race. DC have 10 points from 12 games, while KKR have nine points from 11 games.Mumbai Indians and Lucknow Super Giants are the only two teams eliminated so far in the season.
Article Summary: Royal Challengers Bengaluru reclaim top spot in IPL 2026 points table. Defending champions beat Kolkata Knight Riders by six wickets in Raipur. Virat Kohli scores unbeaten 105 to help RCB chase down 193 in 19.1 overs.

Article Title: 'I was sent home. It’s rage!': Shardul Thakur reopens 2016 Punjab Kings chapter after crushing PBKS playoff hopes - Watch
Article Text:
Shardul Thakur

Already out of the playoffs race, Mumbai Indians defeated Punjab Kings by six wickets in their IPL 2026 match in Dharamsala on Thursday. While MI had nothing to lose, the defeat left Punjab staring at a difficult road in the race for the top four as they suffered their fifth straight loss.After the match, Mumbai Indians pacer Shardul Thakur made a remark that quickly went viral on social media, while speaking about his record against Punjab over the years.In a video shared by Mumbai Indians on social media, a person asked Shardul about his performances against Punjab while playing for different franchises."You have taken so many wickets against Punjab," the person said.To this, Shardul replied, "I was kept out and sent home. It's rage!"Shardul had another strong outing against Punjab on Thursday, finishing with figures of 4 for 39. His spell helped Mumbai pull things back after Punjab made a solid start.Punjab eventually posted 200 for 8 after being asked to bat first. Prabhsimran Singh scored 57, while Priyansh Arya made 27. Shardul dismissed Prabhsimran and skipper Shreyas Iyer in the same spell to put Punjab under pressure.Deepak Chahar (2/36), Corbin Bosch (1/42) and Raj Bawa (1/11) were the other wicket-takers for MI.In reply, Tilak Varma remained unbeaten on 75 off 33 balls as Mumbai chased down the target of 201 with one ball to spare.Will Jacks stayed not out on 25 off 10 balls as MI reached 205 for 4 in 19.5 overs. For Punjab, Azmatullah Omarzai picked up 2 for 36.Shardul’s comment appeared to refer to his time with Kings XI Punjab, now called Punjab Kings.The fast bowler was bought by the franchise in the 2014 IPL auction and remained with them for three seasons. However, he played only one match during that period, against Delhi Daredevils in 2015.During the 2016 IPL season, Punjab released him midway through the tournament. At that time, Shardul had just finished as Mumbai’s highest wicket-taker in their 2015-16 Ranji Trophy-winning season.A frustrated Shardul had then posted on X, formerly Twitter: "Will play a semifinal t20 game tomorrow for my club payyade sc..playing a game after 2 months..ipl has done wonders .. Certainly".
Article Summary: Mumbai Indians defeated Punjab Kings by six wickets in their IPL 2026 match. Shardul Thakur took 4 for 39 as Mumbai reached 205 for 4 in 19.5 overs. Tilak Varma remained unbeaten on 75 as Mumbai chased down the target of 201 with one ball to spare.

Article Title: 'What have you done for Punjab?': Arshdeep Singh sparks fresh row after Tilak Varma controversy
Article Text:
Arshdeep Singh (PTI Photo)

Fresh storm after Tilak remark

Past comments resurface

NEW DELHI: Arshdeep Singh has landed in yet another controversy, with the Punjab Kings pacer facing backlash over a sharp reply to a fan on social media, just as the storm around his remarks involving Tilak Varma showed no signs of fading.The latest episode erupted after a screenshot of an alleged Snapchat exchange surfaced online. A fan had reportedly written, while tagging Arshdeep, to remove Punjab from the IPL franchise’s name, saying he is embarrassing the name.Arshdeep hit back with a blunt response: “What have you done for Punjab? That you are feeling embarrassed, Singh saab? People who still ask family for chips and cold drinks money are now advising whether to use the name or not?”The reply quickly spread across social media, triggering a wave of criticism and reigniting debate over the pacer’s online conduct.The controversy comes barely a day after Arshdeep was accused of making an offensive comment toward Tilak during a video recorded around Punjab’s IPL 2026 clash against Mumbai Indians.In the viral clip, Arshdeep was allegedly heard saying, “Oye Andhere, sunscreen lagaaya?” — a remark many interpreted as referring to Tilak’s skin tone.“Nobody believed me. Everyone mocked and trolled me. Arshdeep should be banned this season and should be paid on a pro rata basis,” Sivaramakrishnan wrote on social media.Sivaramakrishnan said the incident brought back memories from his own playing days, when he claims he faced racial taunts from teammates.He recalled an incident during India’s tour of Pakistan in the 1980s, saying a teammate mocked a birthday cake arranged by Sunil Gavaskar by saying, “You ordered the right colour cake. Such a dark chocolate cake for a dark boy.”Arshdeep’s online activity has drawn scrutiny in recent months. Reports suggested the BCCI had earlier advised him to stop posting vlogs from team tours and match venues during the IPL.Now, with two controversies in as many days, the spotlight on one of India’s leading T20 bowlers has intensified. For Arshdeep, the off-field noise is growing louder — and so are calls for action.
Article Summary: Punjab Kings pacer faces backlash over sharp reply to a fan on social media. Latest episode erupted after a screenshot of an alleged Snapchat exchange surfaced online. A fan had reportedly written, while tagging Arshdeep, to remove Punjab from the IPL franchise’s name, saying he is embarrassing the name.

Article Title: IPL 2026: Rachin Ravindra leaves KKR midway, returns home for New Zealand duty
Article Text:
File Pic: Kolkata Knight Riders' Rachin Ravindra during a practice session. (PTI Photo)

NEW DELHI: Rachin Ravindra has left Kolkata Knight Riders midway through the IPL 2026 season, with the New Zealand batter returning home to prepare for the national side’s upcoming Test tour of England.KKR confirmed the development on Friday, stating that Ravindra had departed the squad to begin his red-ball preparations ahead of New Zealand’s three-match Test series against England, scheduled to begin on June 4.“Rachin Ravindra has returned home to begin his red-ball preparations for New Zealand's upcoming Test tour to England,” the franchise said in an official statement.The franchise also shared a farewell clip on Instagram showing Ravindra exchanging hugs and handshakes with teammates before his departure.“Best wishes from the Knights as Rachin heads home to prepare for national duty,” KKR wrote in the caption.Rachin was bought by KKR for his base price of Rs 2 crore at the IPL 2026 auction after being released by Chennai Super Kings. Despite expectations around the New Zealand all-rounder, he did not play a single game this season.KKR opted for Cameron Green in the middle order role, leaving Ravindra on the bench throughout the campaign.His exit now comes without him making an appearance in the purple and gold.KKR’s season has been a turbulent one. The three-time champions endured a miserable start, failing to register a single win in their first six matches. However, they revived their campaign with four straight victories before losing to Royal Challengers Bengaluru in their previous outing.Led by Ajinkya Rahane , KKR are currently eighth in the points table with nine points from 11 matches and remain on the brink of elimination. They need to win all their remaining matches to stay alive in the playoff race, with net run rate likely to play a decisive role.
Article Summary: Rachin Ravindra has left Kolkata Knight Riders midway through the IPL 2026 season. The New Zealand batter is returning home to prepare for the national side’s upcoming Test tour of England. The three-match Test series against England is scheduled to begin on June 4.

Article Title: IPL 2026 breaks records as reach crosses 1.1 billion amid thrilling playoff race
Article Text:
Fans cheer in the stands during the IPL 2026 match. (PTI Photo)

NEW DELHI: IPL 2026 has recorded its highest-ever audience reach, with the ongoing season crossing a combined 1.1 billion viewers across television and digital platforms as the playoff race heads into a dramatic final stretch.According to figures released by JioStar, the tournament’s continued unpredictability has helped sustain fan engagement through the league phase, driving strong growth across both linear television and digital.The combined reach of 1.1 billion makes IPL 2026 the most widely consumed season in the tournament’s history so far. Linear TV remains strong and is on track to touch the 500-million mark, while digital has emerged as the major growth driver.Digital reach has increased by 15 per cent compared to last season, with watch-time also climbing by seven per cent. Connected TV has seen a sharp rise as well, with reach growing 25 per cent and watch-time increasing by 20 per cent.One of the standout trends has been the growth in regional viewership. Watch-time for regional language feeds has jumped by 42 per cent, underlining the IPL’s deepening footprint across markets beyond English and Hindi audiences.Anup Govindan, Head of Sales at JioStar Sports, said the close playoff race has played a major role in keeping viewership consistently high.“What stands out this IPL season is the consistency of fan engagement right through the league stage across JioStar platforms. The competitive intensity has driven a week-on-week climb in reach, which shows how strongly the tournament is resonating with fans across screens.”The broadcaster also reported a sharp increase in brand participation. A total of 125 new advertisers joined this season compared to IPL 2025. Of those, 22 partnered on digital, while 16 signed up for television, reflecting the tournament’s growing appeal to both legacy and tech-led brands.JioStar’s “Champions Wali Feed” has also become a major attraction. Featuring experts such as Ravichandran Ashwin, Suresh Raina, Harbhajan Singh, Virender Sehwag and Irfan Pathan, the alternate feed has blended live analysis, anecdotes and predictions with match action.With several teams still in contention for the playoffs, every fixture has carried added significance — a factor that appears to have translated directly into record-breaking audience numbers.(Note: Data for 51 matches on LTV, 57 on digital)
Article Summary: IPL 2026 has recorded its highest-ever audience reach, with the ongoing season crossing a combined 1.1 billion viewers. Digital reach has increased by 15 per cent compared to last season, with watch-time also climbing by seven per cent.

Article Title: End of an era: Robin Singh parts ways with Mumbai Indians after 16-year journey
Article Text:
Robin Singh (IANS Photo)

Key role beyond IPL

NEW DELHI: A long chapter in Mumbai Indians history has come to an end, with former India all-rounder Robin Singh officially announcing his departure from the franchise after a 16-year association.Robin confirmed on Friday that he has parted ways with both Mumbai Indians and their overseas sister franchise MI Emirates, bringing to a close one of the longest support-staff stints in franchise cricket.“I’d like to share that I have officially parted ways with the Mumbai Indians. Although I have not been part of their IPL staff since 2022, as of this year also with their team in the Emirates league,” Robin wrote in a social media post.He also thanked the franchise and fans for their support over the years.“Thank you to the entire MI family and the fans for the support through this remarkable journey of mine since 2010. Wishing the franchise good luck in the years ahead.”Robin joined Mumbai Indians in 2010 and became a key figure behind the scenes during the franchise’s most successful era. He was part of the setup during all five IPL title-winning campaigns and also contributed to the team’s two Champions League Twenty20 triumphs.Though his direct role with MI’s IPL unit ended in 2022, Robin remained involved in the wider Mumbai Indians network. He served as general manager of MI Emirates during the inaugural International League T20 season in 2023 and was later elevated as head of the franchise in 2024.His exit marks the end of a significant coaching era for the franchise, where he worked alongside several title-winning squads.Before his coaching career, Robin represented India in 136 ODIs, scoring 2,336 runs and taking 69 wickets. He later became one of the most sought-after coaches in T20 leagues around the world.
Article Summary: Former India all-rounder Robin Singh announces his departure from Mumbai Indians. Robin confirmed on Friday that he has parted ways with both Mumbai Indians and their overseas sister franchise MI Emirates. Robin joined Mumbai Indians in 2010 and became a key figure behind the scenes during the franchise's most successful era.

Article Title: Fact Check: Is UFC planning to outshine buzzing Ronda Rousey vs Gina Carano Netflix bout with Conor McGregor blockbuster return announcement?
Article Text:
Conor McGregor (Image Source: Getty)

UFC could use Conor McGregor announcement to shift attention

The mixed martial arts world is buzzing with speculation that the UFC could announce the return of Conor McGregor on Saturday, May 16, 2026. Fans believe the promotion may use the announcement to steal attention from a major rival event taking place on the same night. The competing show is the Netflix-streamed MVP MMA card, headlined by a blockbuster fight between Ronda Rousey and Gina Carano Rumors have grown stronger after reports suggested the UFC is preparing a promotional campaign around a possible rematch between Conor McGregor and Max Holloway. Many analysts expect the fight to headline UFC 329 during International Fight Week. Fans on social media believe the timing of the announcement is not accidental and could be part of the UFC’s long history of counter-programming against rival promotions.The UFC has often responded aggressively when competing organizations gain momentum. Fans pointed to previous examples, including the promotion’s reaction when the Professional Fighters League signed Francis Ngannou in 2023. At that time, the UFC quickly announced major fights and news to control headlines in the MMA world."Imagine if they drop a 'Conor is back' promo on Saturday night at the same time as the Netflix card.I think it would take some attention away, now I don't know if that's the plan and I don't know if that's what they're thinking of doing, but it would be an interesting move”, Ariel Helwani said on his show.Now, many believe the company may follow a similar strategy again. The rival event this weekend is expected to attract huge interest because of the return of Ronda Rousey and the long-awaited clash with Gina Carano on Netflix.MMA journalist Ariel Helwani recently discussed the possibility during his show. He suggested that releasing a “McGregor is back” promotional video during the Netflix event would fit the UFC’s usual promotional style. Helwani explained that such a move could immediately pull online discussion toward the UFC and reduce some attention on the competing card. However, he also clarified that he does not know whether the company truly plans to do it.While fans continue to debate the rumors, the UFC’s own event on Saturday is UFC Vegas 117. The card will feature a featherweight main event between Arnold Allen and Melquizael Costa. Even so, much of the conversation across the MMA community remains focused on the possible return of Conor McGregor and whether the UFC is preparing another major promotional surprise.
Article Summary: UFC could use Conor McGregor announcement to shift attention from rival event. Rumors have grown stronger after reports suggested the UFC is preparing a promotional campaign around a possible rematch between Conor McGregor and Max Holloway. Fans on social media believe the timing of the announcement is not accidental and could be part of the UFC’s long history of counter-programming.

Article Title: ‘It motivates me’: Akash Singh reveals story behind viral note celebration - Watch
Article Text:
Akash Singh with his viral note celebration. (IPL Photo)

WATCH:

‘It motivates me during the game’

‘You should come to dominate’

NEW DELHI: Akash Singh lit up IPL 2026 on Friday with both ball and emotion, producing a memorable three-wicket spell - 3/26 in 4 overs - for Lucknow Super Giants against Chennai Super Kings before explaining the note that turned his celebration viral.Playing his first game of the season, the left-arm pacer struck three times, dismissing Ruturaj Gaikwad Sanju Samson and Urvil Patel. After every wicket, he pulled out a folded chit from his pocket and flashed it around the stadium, sparking curiosity.Akash later revealed there was no hidden message — it was simply self-motivation.“I know I express myself well and people tell me that too. But honestly, it’s about the environment you create around yourself. I spend time understanding how champions think and how champions work hard,” Akash told broadcasters during the mid-innings interview.He added that the note contained words meant to fuel his own confidence.“On that note I had written things like ‘Akki knows how to take wickets in T20 cricket’. It just motivates me during the game. Sometimes these things work, sometimes they don’t, but if something helps me stay motivated and helps me perform better, then I’m willing to do it,” Akash said.The celebration reflected the emotion of a player finally getting his chance after waiting all season.Akash said his mindset was built around attacking the biggest names.“My mindset is always about getting big wickets because when you come to the IPL, you shouldn’t just come to play — you should come to dominate,” he said.The 24-year-old said his preparation focused equally on skill and mentality.“You need to stay one step ahead skill-wise, but at the same time the mental side matters a lot. If you don’t dominate mentally in this game, you can’t move forward consistently,” he added.Having not featured all season, Akash said the performance was a reward for persistence.“When you are not playing regularly in the IPL, you have to keep pushing yourself constantly. Throughout the season and even in the off-season, I kept working hard,” he concluded.
Article Summary: Akash Singh took three wickets for Lucknow Super Giants against Chennai Super Kings. After every wicket, he pulled out a folded chit from his pocket and flashed it around the stadium, sparking curiosity. Akash later revealed there was no hidden message — it was simply self-motivation.

Article Title: ‘Not comfortable’: Virat Kohli slams invasive camera culture after Kane Williamson chat turns into a ‘moment’
Article Text:
Virat Kohli and Kane Williamson (Agency Image)

Poll Do you think cricketers should have the right to practice without being filmed? Yes, they deserve privacy. No, filming should be allowed.

Virat Kohli has spoken candidly about the growing presence of cameras around players during the Indian Premier League, admitting that the constant monitoring during practice sessions has become uncomfortable and intrusive. The Royal Challengers Bengaluru star said players should be allowed to prepare in peace without every movement being recorded and analysed on social media.One of the most followed cricketers in the world, Kohli remains under constant attention both on and off the field. From training drills to casual interactions, nearly every moment involving the former India captain quickly finds its way online. While acknowledging the importance of fan engagement and digital content in modern cricket, Kohli believes things have now reached a stage where there needs to be a balance.Speaking on the RCB Podcast, Kohli explained how difficult it is to focus freely on improving his game when multiple cameras are tracking every activity during practice.“You walk to practice, and you have six cameras following you. It’s not a comfortable feeling at all. As a sports person, you need the ability and the freedom to work on your game in peace. If everything you do is an opportunity to film or display or to dissect, then you are not being organic," Kohli said.The 37-year-old added that players often experiment with techniques or methods during practice, but the fear of those moments being circulated publicly changes the way they train.“I’m not going to be able to try to do things at practice which I really want to do because I know tomorrow if someone films it and there are discussions on my practice sessions. You judge me on my performances during the game, not during the leadup. No one has the right to judge me on what I’m doing in terms of my preparation, the things I’m trying in the nets,” he added.Kohli further stated that while social media has become a major part of the IPL ecosystem and team branding, there must be clearer boundaries regarding player consent and access behind the scenes.“I just feel like there has to be a bit more streamlining in terms of understanding how much to do, when to do, is the player okay with being filmed at all times. Those kinds of things really need to be taken into account because it gets too much. I love the pressure of the game, but I honestly don’t love the pressure of anything else. Like social media is a huge part of the commercial representation or the fan engagement for any team, which is understandable," Kohli said.The batting great also pointed out that the explosion of official fan pages and social media content around franchises happened gradually over time, meaning players were never fully prepared for this level of constant exposure from the beginning of the IPL.“But I genuinely feel like there has to be a bit more streamlining, because if you look at the growth of the official fan clubs or the official fan pages of teams, it has taken place after a long period of time when the IPL was already in play. So it’s not a thing that people were ready for from day one,” he added.Kohli also recalled a recent interaction with Kane Williamson during RCB’s IPL 2026 clash against the Lucknow Super Giants, where even a private conversation became difficult because of cameras and the IPL’s roaming Champak robot.“I was talking to Kane the other day, and there’s that robot thing just waving at me. And I am like, ‘Why is this thing waving at me?’ I’m talking to Kane about something so serious. First, I ignored the dog, and then I told the man controlling it, please take him away. Let me talk in peace. Kane’s my friend. I can’t have a conversation with him without it becoming a ‘moment’,” said Kohli.Despite his concerns away from the field, Kohli has enjoyed another outstanding IPL campaign with the bat. The RCB talisman recently smashed his ninth IPL century against Kolkata Knight Riders and has so far scored 484 runs in 12 matches this season at an average of 53.78 and a strike rate of 165.75.
Article Summary: Virat Kohli has spoken candidly about the growing presence of cameras around players during the Indian Premier League. The Royal Challengers Bengaluru star said players should be allowed to prepare in peace without every movement being recorded and analysed on social media. Kohli also recalled a recent interaction with Kane Williamson during RCB’s IPL 2026 clash against the Lucknow Super Giants.

Article Title: Jasprit Bumrah ends 156-match wait: ‘I didn’t think I’d be India captain before MI captain’
Article Text:
Jasprit Bumrah (IPL Photo)

A long wait before first captaincy

Most matches in IPL before leading a side

200 - Ravindra Jadeja

156 - Jasprit Bumrah

153 - Manish Pandey

148 - Axar Patel

137 - Kieron Pollard

126 - Suryakumar Yadav

Bumrah’s message despite MI’s difficult season

NEW DELHI: Jasprit Bumrah finally led Mumbai Indians for the first time on Thursday, and his first reaction captured just how unexpected the moment was.“I didn’t think that I’ll be a Test captain before I’ll captain Mumbai Indians,” Bumrah said with a smile at the toss before MI’s IPL 2026 clash against Punjab Kings in Dharamsala.The fast bowler stepped in as captain with regular skipper Hardik Pandya unavailable due to a back spasm, while stand-in captain Suryakumar Yadav missed the game for personal reasons.Bumrah’s captaincy debut came after 156 IPL matches for MI, making him the second-most experienced player to lead an IPL side for the first time.Only Ravindra Jadeja had waited longer, taking charge only after 200 matches. Bumrah moved ahead of Manish Pandey (153), Axar Patel (148), Kieron Pollard (137) and even teammate Suryakumar (126).He also became the 10th player to captain Mumbai Indians in franchise’s history, adding another chapter to a season already filled with unusual changes for the five-time champions.With MI struggling near the bottom of the points table with just three wins from 11 matches, Bumrah said the team still wanted to embrace the final stretch rather than write the season off.“When cricket gives you good days, you take it. When it gives you challenges, it’s come to teach you something,” he said.He added that the side was determined to enjoy the remaining three matches and “ruffle a few teams” before the campaign ends.“I like responsibility. This is a new challenge. I’ll look to have fun,” Bumrah said.That summed up the occasion. After more than a decade with MI and 156 games in blue, one of the franchise’s greatest bowlers finally had the captain’s armband.
Article Summary: Jasprit Bumrah captained Mumbai Indians for the first time on Thursday. The fast bowler stepped in with regular skipper Hardik Pandya unavailable due to a back spasm. Stand-in captain Suryakumar Yadav missed the game for personal reasons. He became the 10th player to captain Mumbai Indians in franchise's history.

Article Title: Deepak Chahar's reaction after deceiving Priyansh Arya with variation in MI vs PBKS match goes viral - Watch
Article Text:
Photo/screengrab

Deepak Chahar dismissed Priyansh Arya after setting him up with a planned sequence of deliveries, during the IPL 2026 match between Mumbai Indians and Punjab Kings in Dharamsala on Thursday.In the 58th match of the tournament, Mumbai Indians were led by Jasprit Bumrah, who stepped in as captain with Hardik Pandya and Suryakumar Yadav unavailable. Bumrah won the toss and chose to field first.Punjab Kings openers Priyansh Arya and Prabhsimran Singh put together a 50-run partnership before Chahar got the breakthrough in the final over of the powerplay.On the second ball of the over, Chahar bowled a fast inswinging yorker from around the wicket. The ball moved sharply towards the leg stump at high pace and nearly hit it. Priyansh was beaten and moved his leg away, and the ball went for four byes.On the next ball, Chahar kept the same action and setup but changed the pace, bowling a slower knuckle ball. Priyansh expected another quick inswinger and went for a lofted drive. He played early, and the ball went through to hit the leg stump.The celebration explained everything as Deepak Chahar knew the previous ball had already set the batter up.In the match, Prabhsimran Singh smashed a fluent half-century before Shardul Thakur’s four-wicket burst triggered a dramatic collapse, but a strong late surge helped Punjab Kings post a competitive 200 for 8 against Mumbai Indians.Asked to bat first after suffering four successive defeats, PBKS rode on an attacking 33-ball 50-run opening stand between Priyansh Arya (27) and Prabhsimran (57).
Article Summary: Punjab Kings openers Priyansh Arya and Prabhsimran Singh put together a 50-run partnership. Mumbai Indians were led by Jasprit Bumrah, who stepped in as captain with Hardik Pandya and Suryakumar Yadav unavailable. Shardul Thakur’s four-wicket burst triggered a dramatic collapse.

Article Title: ‘Rule needs to change ASAP’: Ashwin slams DRS after late drama in PBKS vs MI clash
Article Text:
Punjab Kings and Mumbai Indians players during the match. (IPL Photo)

Final-ball controversy sparks debate

Punjab recover to post 200

NEW DELHI: Ravichandran Ashwin has called for an urgent change to the DRS rules after a controversial final-ball incident during the IPL 2026 clash between Punjab Kings and Mumbai Indians in Dharamsala on Thursday.The former India spinner took to social media after Punjab were denied a leg-bye despite successfully overturning an lbw decision on review in the final ball of their innings.“This wrong decision has cost Punjab a run, this rule needs to change ASAP! Just like how the Impact Sub is an IPL rule, the DRS reversal should also be brought into the IPL even if the ICC delays it for some reason,” Ashwin posted.The incident came when Jasprit Bumrah trapped Vishnu Vinod lbw with the final delivery of Punjab’s innings. The on-field umpire raised his finger, but the batter reviewed immediately.Replays showed the ball was missing the stumps, and the decision was reversed. However, because the umpire had initially given him out, the ball had already been declared dead, meaning Punjab were not awarded the leg-bye they had completed.Ashwin pointed out that the incorrect on-field call effectively cost Punjab one run, reigniting discussion over whether the IPL should modify the playing condition independently.The incident capped a dramatic innings in which Punjab posted 200 for 8 after a collapse threatened to derail them.Prabhsimran Singh starred with 57 and shared a brisk opening stand with Priyansh Arya. But Mumbai struck back through Shardul Thakur, who claimed four wickets to trigger a middle-order collapse.With Punjab slipping badly, Azmatullah Omarzai launched a late counterattack with 38 off 17 balls, while Vishnu Vinod and Xavier Bartlett added crucial runs at the death.
Article Summary: Ravichandran Ashwin has called for an urgent change to the DRS rules. The former India spinner took to social media after Punjab were denied a leg-bye despite overturning an lbw decision on review. The incident came when Jasprit Bumrah trapped Vishnu Vinod lbw with the final delivery of Punjab’s innings.

Article Title: Ipl Qualification Chances: Rain threat looms large over PBKS' IPL 2026 playoffs chances! What happens if match is washed out vs MI
Article Text:
Rain threat looms over PBKS' IPL 2026 Playoff chances (Image: IPL/BCCI)

Watch Delhi Capitals' Madhav Tiwari reflects on his brilliant all-round performance Delhi Capitals' Madhav Tiwari reflects on his brilliant all-round performance

What it means for Punjab Kings (PBKS)



What it means for Mumbai Indians (MI)



Punjab Kings face Mumbai Indians in a must-win clash to strengthen their playoff hopes, but a looming rain threat in Dharamshala during match time could impact Punjab Kings’ chances.The Dharamshala weather forecast indicates a high 55% to 60% chance of rain and thunderstorms for tonight's match, making a rain interruption or a complete washout highly possible. The Indian Meteorological Department has issued a 'Yellow Alert' for the Kangra Valley, as heavy downpours and overcast skies have already disrupted pre-match practice sessions at the HPCA Stadium.Punjab Kings currently sit in 4th place with 13 points and a Net Run Rate (NRR) of +0.428 from 11 matches. They are closely pursued by Chennai Super Kings (+0.185 NRR) and Rajasthan Royals (+0.082 NRR), who both have 12 points.If the IPL 2026 clash in Dharamsala is washed out due to rain, both teams will receive one point each, resulting in a “No Result.” However, with Mumbai Indians already mathematically eliminated from the playoff race, the shared points would have very different consequences for the two sides.A washout would take PBKS to 14 points, helping them maintain a slight cushion over chasing teams like Chennai Super Kings and Rajasthan Royals, who are both currently on 12 points.However, the bigger concern for Punjab would be the impact on their top-two hopes.A no-result would cap their maximum possible points tally at 18 if they go on to win their remaining two matches against Royal Challengers Bengaluru and Lucknow Super Giants. That would significantly reduce their chances of overtaking table-toppers RCB and Gujarat Titans, both of whom already sit on 16 points. It would also mark Punjab’s second abandoned game of the season, making them the only team in the current top four relying on split points rather than outright victories.For Mumbai Indians, who are already out of playoff contention, a washout would mainly deny them the opportunity to play spoiler against a direct rival in the playoff race. The single point would, however, move MI slightly ahead of Lucknow Super Giants in the lower half of the table, marginally helping their effort to avoid finishing at the bottom of the standings.
Article Summary: Punjab Kings currently sit in 4th place with 13 points and a Net Run Rate (NRR) of +0.428 from 11 matches. A washout would take PBKS to 14 points, helping them maintain a slight cushion over chasing teams like Chennai Super Kings and Rajasthan Royals, who are both on 12 points. Mumbai Indians are already mathematically eliminated from the playoff race.

Article Title: Micah Parsons may miss multiple games for the Packers as Patrick Mahomes gets a much better recovery update
Article Text:
Patrick Mahomes could return Week 1 after faster ACL Surgery than Micah Parsons

The Green Bay Packers may start the season without Micah Parsons after a major PUP list update



What the PUP list actually means

Parsons vs. Mahomes — Same injury, very different updates



Green Bay's most important player might not even suit up for Week 1. Not great. Adam Schefter dropped a brutal update Thursday morning on ESPN's Get Up. Parsons is officially "a candidate to be placed on the Physically Unable to Perform List" to start the season, as he continues to recover from his torn ACL. The Packers gave up a king's ransom for this man. This is not what they were hoping to hear.Parsons went down with a season-ending ACL tear on December 14, prematurely closing out his first season in Green Bay. He'd been everything the Packers wanted, 12.5 sacks in 14 games, leading the team by a wide margin. Then it all fell apart. Without him, the Packers collapsed down the stretch, eventually losing a Wild Card game to division rivals the Chicago Bears after blowing an 18-point lead. Rough.For the uninitiated, it's not great. Being placed on the PUP list at the beginning of the season means Parsons would have to miss at least the first four games. There is a small silver lining, though. The NFL modified its PUP rule at the league's annual meeting, now allowing players to begin a 21-day practice period after the club's second game of the regular season, down from four games previously. So theoretically, Parsons could be back on the practice field by Week 3.Parsons himself has previously said he expects to return around Week 3 or 4, with a potential Thursday Night Football home game against the Atlanta Falcons or a road game against Tampa Bay as his target. His most realistic return? Week 5 against the Bears. Revenge game incoming.Mahomes suffered a torn ACL and torn LCL on the same day as Parsons, December 14. Same injury day. Completely different recovery story. Schefter says Mahomes has a "realistic chance" of being ready for Week 1, with the Chiefs' coaching staff telling people around the league they expect him to be fully cleared early in training camp.Parsons is flying through rehab in Italy. Mahomes is apparently just built different.Green Bay gave up two first-round picks and Kenny Clark to land Parsons, who then signed a four-year, $188 million deal with $136 million guaranteed. Starting the season without him is a massive blow to a defense still trying to prove it belongs.The Packers are being cautious. Smart. But Packers fans? They are not okay.
Article Summary: The Green Bay Packers may start the season without Micah Parsons. Parsons went down with a season-ending ACL tear on December 14. The Packers gave up two first-round picks and Kenny Clark to land Parsons, who then signed a four-year, $188 million deal with $136 million guaranteed.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Steve Jobs: “Stay hungry, stay foolish”
Article Text:
Steve Jobs (Image: Wikipedia)

Quote of the day by Steve Jobs

“Stay hungry, stay foolish”

Where did Steve Jobs say “Stay hungry, stay foolish”

“Stay hungry. Stay foolish.”

Why the quote still resonates with younger generations



What did Steve Jobs really mean by “hungry”



Why “stay foolish” may be the more important part



The quote also reflects Steve Jobs’ personality



Why this quote is so popular



The quote quietly pushes against comfort culture



Why the quote still feels emotional years after Jobs’ death



Why “Stay hungry, stay foolish” still matters today







Some quotes become famous because they sound motivational. Others survive because they seem to describe an entire mindset in just a few words. Steve Jobs ’ famous line, “Stay hungry, stay foolish,” belongs in the second category.The sentence is incredibly short - just four words.Yet decades later, people still print it on posters, repeat it during graduation speeches, post it online before career changes, and mention it whenever conversations turn toward ambition, creativity, or taking risks in life.Part of the reason may be that the quote feels unfinished in an interesting way. Jobs does not fully explain what “hungry” means. He does not clearly define “foolish” either. Readers almost complete the meaning themselves, depending on where they are in life.A student might hear courage in it. An entrepreneur may hear risk-taking. Someone stuck in an unhappy routine may hear permission to start again.That flexibility gave the quote unusual staying power over the years.And honestly, it still feels strangely relevant now, perhaps even more than when Jobs originally said it.Steve Jobs famously used the phrase during his 2005 speech at Stanford University, a speech that later became one of the most widely watched graduation speeches ever delivered.Near the end of the speech, Jobs explained that the line originally appeared in the final issue of The Whole Earth Catalog, a counterculture publication from the 1960s and 1970s known for promoting creativity, independent thinking, curiosity, and experimentation.The magazine ended with the words:Jobs said he had always considered it a powerful piece of advice. Years later, those four words became permanently associated with him.Interestingly, the line feels very different from traditional corporate advice. Most business language focuses on stability, certainty, strategy, and careful planning. Jobs’ phrase almost celebrates discomfort instead. Hunger suggests dissatisfaction. Foolishness suggests risk, uncertainty, and willingness to appear wrong publicly.That combination feels unusual even today.A lot of older motivational quotes slowly fade because they become too tied to one historical moment. “Stay hungry, stay foolish” somehow avoided that problem.Younger audiences continue connecting with it because modern life often feels uncertain already. Career paths are less predictable now. Technology changes constantly. Entire industries appear and disappear quickly. Many people no longer expect a perfectly stable future.In that environment, Jobs’ advice sounds less reckless and more realistic.The phrase “stay hungry” seems to encourage curiosity and ambition rather than comfort. It pushes against complacency. People who stop learning, experimenting, or questioning things often become stagnant professionally and creatively.The “stay foolish” part may be even more important.Fear of embarrassment stops many people from trying new things. Adults often become cautious because they worry about looking naïve, inexperienced, or unrealistic. Jobs almost flips that fear upside down. He suggests foolishness may actually be necessary for creativity and innovation.Without foolish ideas, genuinely new ideas may never appear at all.The word “hungry” in this quote clearly goes beyond physical hunger.Jobs seem to mean intellectual hunger. Creative hunger. Emotional restlessness. The refusal to become too satisfied too early in life.That mindset shaped much of his career.Even after enormous success with Apple , Jobs remained obsessed with improving products, changing industries, and rethinking existing ideas. Sometimes that intensity made him difficult personally. Former colleagues often described him as demanding and relentless. Yet the same mindset also helped create products that transformed technology and popular culture globally.The quote reflects that personality closely.Jobs appeared deeply uncomfortable with stagnation. He believed people should keep learning, questioning, exploring, and imagining possibilities even after becoming successful.That idea explains why the quote continues to appeal strongly to ambitious people today. Many individuals fear becoming trapped inside routines that feel emotionally or creatively deadening. “Stay hungry” almost sounds like a warning against settling too comfortably into predictable patterns.Interestingly, the second half of the quote often affects people more deeply over time.“Stay foolish” sounds strange because society usually teaches the opposite lesson. Schools reward correct answers. Workplaces reward expertise and competence. Adults are encouraged to avoid mistakes and appear knowledgeable.Jobs suggests something very different.He implies that creativity often requires a willingness to look foolish temporarily. Many groundbreaking ideas initially sound unrealistic or impractical. People experimenting with something genuinely new rarely appear completely confident in the beginning.That applies far beyond business.Someone changing careers later in life may feel foolish initially. Starting a creative project can feel foolish, too. Moving to a new place. Learning new skills. Taking emotional risks in relationships. Most meaningful changes involve uncertainty and vulnerability somewhere along the way.Jobs seems to argue that the fear of looking foolish traps people emotionally and creatively.And honestly, many people probably recognise that feeling immediately.One reason this line became so iconic is that it genuinely sounded like Steve Jobs himself.Apple Inc. co-founder Jobs built much of his public image around unconventional thinking. He famously dropped out of college, experimented with artistic and spiritual influences, challenged traditional corporate culture, and pushed products many people initially doubted.Some of his decisions looked irrational at the time.The iPhone seemed risky before smartphones dominated daily life. Apple Stores were heavily criticised before becoming massively successful. Even Pixar looked uncertain initially before transforming animated filmmaking.Jobs repeatedly pursued ideas others considered unrealistic or commercially dangerous.That history gives the quote credibility. It did not sound like generic motivation coming from him. It reflected how he actually approached life and work.The internet helped turn “Stay hungry, stay foolish” into something larger than a graduation quote.Partly because the line works almost everywhere.It fits the entrepreneurship culture. Creative culture. Student life. Career advice. Self-improvement discussions. Technology communities. Even personal growth conversations.The wording also feels emotionally open-ended enough for people to project their own experiences onto it. Someone feeling stuck professionally may read encouragement into it. Someone afraid of failure may hear reassurance instead.That flexibility made the quote endlessly shareable online.At the same time, the phrase avoids sounding overly polished. Jobs did not overload it with complicated explanations or corporate language. The simplicity probably helped it survive culturally for so long.Modern culture often sends mixed messages about success. People are encouraged to dream big while simultaneously avoiding risk. Social media especially creates pressure to appear successful immediately. Failure becomes public very quickly online, which makes many individuals even more cautious about trying uncertain things.Jobs’ quote pushes in the opposite direction.“Stay hungry” suggests refusing comfort entirely. “Stay foolish” suggests accepting uncertainty as part of growth.That combination feels emotionally liberating for many readers because it removes the expectation of perfect certainty before action. Jobs almost treats experimentation itself as valuable even when outcomes remain unclear.Steve Jobs died in 2011, yet this quote still circulates constantly online, especially among younger audiences who were children or not even born during much of Apple’s rise.That says something interesting.The line survived beyond the person who said it because it captures a larger human tension that people continue experiencing. Most individuals want security, but they also fear becoming emotionally or creatively stagnant. They want stability while also wanting growth and excitement.Jobs’ quote acknowledges that conflict indirectly. It suggests meaningful life may require maintaining some restlessness permanently.Not destructive dissatisfaction. But curiosity. Ambition. The willingness to remain open to change.Some famous quotes disappear because they depend too heavily on trends or temporary cultural moods. Steve Jobs’ line continues surviving because the emotional tension inside it never really goes away.People still fear failure. They still worry about looking foolish publicly. They still become trapped inside routines that feel safe but are emotionally unfulfilling. And many still quietly wonder whether they are capable of doing something larger, stranger, or more meaningful with their lives.That is probably why these four words continue resonating so strongly.Jobs was not promising easy success. If anything, the quote almost accepts uncertainty as unavoidable. Hunger means remaining unsatisfied enough to keep exploring. Foolishness means staying open to possibilities other people may dismiss initially.Perhaps that mindset feels comforting because it removes the pressure to have everything figured out perfectly.Curiosity becomes enough. Trying becomes enough. Remaining open to change becomes enough.And honestly, in a world where so many people feel pressured to appear certain all the time, that message still feels surprisingly freeing.
Article Summary: Steve Jobs famously used the phrase during his 2005 speech at Stanford University. The line originally appeared in The Whole Earth Catalog, a counterculture publication from the 1960s and 1970s. The phrase “stay hungry” seems to encourage curiosity and ambition rather than comfort.
